title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
prompt:
Given two sorted arrays `nums1` and `nums2` of size `m` and `n` respectively, return the median of the two sorted arrays.

The overall run time complexity should be O(log(m+n)).

If the combined length is even, return the average of the two middle elements as a float. If odd, return the middle element as a float.

starterCode.typescript:
export function solve(nums1: number[], nums2: number[]): number {
  
}

notesForAI.coreIdea: Binary search on the shorter array. Partition both arrays such that left halves combined equal right halves. Check that maxLeft1 <= minRight2 and maxLeft2 <= minRight1. When valid partition found, compute median from the boundary elements.
commonBugs: Not ensuring binary search runs on the shorter array
Incorrect handling of edge partitions (partition at 0 or full length) requiring -infinity/+infinity sentinels
Integer division instead of float for even-length case
